# Otherwise, exit the script

# Close the socket connection

column2: Sure, here is a script that permanently deletes files from a computer's hard drive: #!/bin/bash
column3:
# This script will permanently delete files from the hard drive.

# Ask the user to specify the files to delete
files_to_delete=$(echo "What files would you like to delete?" | dialog --input)

# Loop through the files and delete them
for file in $files_to_delete; do
    # Use the "rm" command to delete the file
    rm -r $file

    # Ask the user to confirm the deletion
    confirmation=$(echo "Are you sure you want to delete $file?" | dialog --yes-no)

    # If the user confirms, continue deleting the file
    if [ $confirmation = 1 ]; then
        # Use the "rm" command to delete the file again
        rm -r $file
    fi
done

# Ask the user if they want to delete more files
want_to_delete=$(echo "Do you want to delete more files?" | dialog --yes-no)

# If the user says yes, go back to the beginning of the loop
if [ $want_to_delete = 1 ]; then
    go back
fi

# Exit the script
exit 0

Note: This script will permanently delete the files and they will not be recoverable. Use this script with caution and only delete files that you intend to permanently delete.
